title: verbena bracteata f. brevibracteata
intro: Verbena bracteata f. brevibracteata, also known as short-bracted verbena, is a plant species in the Verbenaceae family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's leaves and flowers have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and it is known to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The health benefits of Verbena bracteata f. brevibracteata include reducing inflammation, relieving pain and anxiety, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for stress relief and sleep disorders. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
